howItWorks.intro: This power factor calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Apparent Power (kVA) = Real Power (kW) / Power Factor
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is power factor?
faq.answer: Power factor is the ratio of real power (kW) to apparent power (kVA). A PF of 1.0 is perfect efficiency. Most commercial buildings have PF of 0.80-0.95. Low PF means wasted energy.

howWeCalculate.intro: This power factor calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs

Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.
